regimeSub: CPI at 3.5% holds below May's 4.2% peak; Kimi K3 shock dents Korea semis but AI capex thesis intact.
themes: June CPI 3.5% YoY confirms NY Fed Williams 'inflation peaked' thesis → 2y yield 4.18% steady, July FOMC hike odds suppressed despite Dallas Fed Logan's 'modestly higher' rate call (Jul 16)
BOK 25bp hike to 2.75% (first in 3.5 yrs) digested cleanly → USDKRW 1479 stable, won at 2-month highs per RTTNews as AI chip boom stokes domestic inflation
Samsung/SK Hynix sell off on Kimi K3 shock (Jul 20) but TSMC $100bn US capex + raised guidance (Jul 16-17) keeps HBM supply crisis narrative intact through 2027
Brent $88.64 off highs as OPEC+ August quota hike offsets renewed Hormuz hostilities → gold $4027 at ATH on Trump Iran sanctions push (Jul 19), VIX 18.65 muted

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10y 4.55% minus 3.5% CPI = +105bp real rate; restrictive but stable as Williams sees inflation peaked

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2s10s at +37bp; steepener intact as front-end anchored on Fed hold, long end reflects fiscal/supply

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads contained as SPX 7443 holds near highs; risk appetite intact despite Hormuz flare-up

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: VIX 18.65 muted given geopolitical noise; complacency on Fed pause + soft landing narrative

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $88.64 retreating from $90 as OPEC+ quota hike offsets Hormuz supply risk

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: Gold $4027 ATH; geopolitical hedge bid on Trump Iran sanctions + Hormuz tensions

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: USDKRW 1479 stable post-BOK hike; won at 2-month high, AI chip earnings supportive

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Kimi K3 shock hits Samsung/SK Hynix Monday; TSMC capex raise keeps sector bid intact

headline: Disinflation thesis intact as June CPI holds at 3.5% below May's 4.2% peak; Korea semis digest Kimi K3 shock while TSMC capex and BOK hike anchor Asia risk amid persistent Hormuz tail risk.
